title: feat(registry): add model titles while preserving descriptions
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Adds required `title` metadata to model registry entries and exposes it through model catalog surfaces.
- Preserves existing title-prefixed `description` strings for backward compatibility; internal display helpers strip the prefix where needed.
- Adds/exports `maxReferenceImages`, `maxReferenceVideos`, and video capability metadata for registry-driven UI limits.
- Updates SDK/UI consumers to read `title`; SDK catalog entries require registry `title` instead of falling back to `name`.
- Keeps Mistral routing on `mistral-small-2503`; follow-up verification is tracked in #11633.

Checks:
- `packages/sdk`: `npm test -- --run src/models.test.ts`, `npm run typecheck`
- `gen.pollinations.ai`: `npm run typecheck`
- Biome on touched files

gist.headline: Name tags for the hive
gist.blurb: We've added proper titles and capability metadata to all our models in the registry. Now your favorite AI models have clean, readable names in the UI without breaking any existing descriptions.
gist.summary: Added a dedicated `title` field and capability metadata like `maxReferenceImages` and `maxReferenceVideos` to the model registry, updating the SDK and UI components to display clean model names.
gist.impact: Developers using the SDK and users browsing the UI will now see cleaner, properly formatted model titles and accurate capability limits for image and video references.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee acting as a librarian or archivist, putting clean new name tags on jars or books (representing models), with the Robot helping out by holding a clipboard.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny librarian's cardigan, carefully placing neat, glowing name tags onto a row of colorful glass jars on a wooden shelf. A chunky pixel robot with a CRT monitor head stands nearby holding a clipboard, its screen showing a lime green (#ecf874) checklist. Soft pastel gradient background, warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
